message: Hello! I'm your BPMN Modeling Agent. I'll analyze the task now and start planning the diagram.
I've analyzed the task and defined 7 goals for the model.

Setting up all participants (pools) first: HR Portal as the main expanded process owner; Employee, Direct Supervisor, and HR Manager as external (collapsed) participants; HR Department as an expanded participant that receives the request from the portal.

I will now model the full end-to-end process across the existing pools, including the day-balance check, supervisor decision with 5-business-day escalation to HR Manager, booking on approval only, and final notifications to the Employee. I’ll also include the ‘request arrives at HR Department’ routing as a distinct message exchange.
I've reviewed the completed model. Found 10 issue(s) — details below.
⚠️ 2 critical issue(s) detected. Automatically starting correction pass 1/3…

I’ll fix both event-based gateways by removing the duplicate message triggers into the same IntermediateCatchEvents. The EventBasedGateways will remain as the single waiting mechanism; the supervisor/manager responses will be modeled as messages sent to the gateway (via the collapsed pool), not directly to the catch events.
I've reviewed the completed model. Found 5 issue(s) — details below.
⚠️ 4 critical issue(s) detected. Automatically starting correction pass 2/3…

Fixing the critical BPMN compliance issues: message flows must not target EventBasedGateways. I will retarget the supervisor/manager message flows to the corresponding message IntermediateCatchEvents and remove the invalid message flows to the gateways.
I've reviewed the completed model. Found 4 issue(s) — details below.
⚠️ 4 critical issue(s) detected. Automatically starting correction pass 3/3…
Iteration 1 — fixing the critical message-flow source issues by expanding the Direct Supervisor and HR Manager pools and adding explicit message senders for approve/reject responses.
I've reviewed the completed model. Found 8 issue(s) — details below.
⚠️ After 3 correction attempt(s), 1 critical issue(s) remain. Please review manually.
